architectReview.evidence: Leader audit confirms the G002 ingest/provenance implementation keeps the approved national-average-only boundary intact, persists immutable ingest/revision/snapshot lineage in Prisma-backed tables, and uses a serialized worker lane to avoid overlapping ingest writes. Repeat-run unchanged handling and external-indicator upsert behavior were revalidated against a fresh embedded Postgres database without requiring any out-of-plan behavior.

executorQa.evidence: Current-state verification passed for raw Opinet fetch persistence, Prisma schema health, and a live embedded-Postgres verifier that exercised serialized worker ingest twice plus overwrite-safe external-indicator upserts.
redTeamCommands: Repeat fetch:opinet must keep same-date national-average diesel JSON at one row instead of duplicating it.
Second serialized worker run must report unchangedRowCount=1 and createdRevisionCount=0 for the same source row.
Second indicator sync must update existing rows in place rather than create duplicate history rows.
Latest current-truth row must remain attached to the newest recompute snapshot after the repeat run.

artifactRefs.description: Earlier G002 verification log covering initial fetch, Prisma sync, worker ingest, repeat worker handling, and indicator sync against an embedded Postgres verification server.
Fresh G005 blocker-resolution verification log for current-state fetch persistence, Prisma checks, and the embedded-Postgres worker/indicator verifier.
Structured output from the live embedded-Postgres verifier for worker ingest lineage, repeat-run unchanged handling, and indicator upsert behavior.

contractCoverage.obligation: The raw Opinet daily JSON series updates the same date in place and does not duplicate the national-average diesel row.
Worker ingest persists immutable ingest runs, revision lineage, current truth, and recompute snapshots for the national-average dataset.
The worker executes ingest through one serialized dataset lane so repeated runs share the same advisory-lock domain.
External indicator history uses overwrite-safe upserts keyed by indicatorCode and observedAt.

surfaceEvidence.invocation: npm run fetch:opinet twice + node --import tsx --input-type=module embedded-postgres verifier

adversarialCases.scenario: Run fetch:opinet twice against the current repo state.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The same trade date remains a single row in data/oil-price-daily.json and is updated in place rather than duplicated.

adversarialCases.scenario: Run the serialized worker twice against the same deterministic source row in a fresh embedded Postgres verification database.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The second run must report unchangedRowCount=1 and createdRevisionCount=0 while keeping one current-truth row and a shared serialized lane key.

adversarialCases.scenario: Inspect the latest current-truth row after the repeat worker run.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The row must reference the latest successful recompute snapshot id instead of leaving lineage detached.

adversarialCases.scenario: Sync the same indicator timestamps twice with changed values/source payloads.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The second sync updates the existing rows in place (updatedCount=2) and leaves indicatorRows=2 instead of creating duplicates.

iteration.evidence: The original G002 implementation was not missing behavior; it was blocked by unusable review-lane receipts. This rerun rebuilt the verification evidence from current repo state and re-proved fetch persistence, serialized worker ingest, provenance tables, repeat-run unchanged handling, and external-indicator upserts cleanly.
